intro: Nepeta cataria, also known as Catnip or Nephoanthus prostratus, is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae) that has been used for centuries in folk medicine. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and menstrual cramps. The bioactive constituents of Nepeta cataria include nepetalactone, a volatile oil responsible for its sedative effects, as well as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Nepeta cataria is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, often in combination with other herbs like chamomile and valerian root, to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality.
